description_long: This object consists of four simple, straight-backed chairs arranged side by side, each with a slightly different color scheme. The chairs feature rectangular seats and ladder-style backrests with three horizontal slats. The legs are straight and unadorned, with no armrests present, and the construction appears to be utilitarian, likely made from painted wood or metal with flat, solid seats. The colors range from muted browns and grays to a teal blue, giving a mismatched, urban, or communal vibe. The design is minimalistic, with no visible upholstery or cushioning, and the seats are flat and plain. These chairs are typical of those found in public parks, community centers, or outdoor gathering spaces.
description: A set of four simple, straight-backed chairs with flat seats and ladder-style backs in various muted colors, lacking armrests and upholstery.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four chairs side by side, highlighting their different seat and frame colors and the ladder-style backrests.
description_view_1: Side view showing the profile of the chairs, emphasizing the flatness of the seats and the straight, vertical backrests.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the chairs, with the horizontal slats of the backrests clearly visible and the color differences in the frames.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the profile of the chairs and the alignment of the legs and seats.
